<<<KOT:hydrogel>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Hydrogel is a technical materials-science concept defined by specific physical properties (biphasic composition, polymer network structure, water absorption capacity, permeability) that require chemical and polymer nomenclature outside the prime lexicon. While one could gesture at 'a thing made of solid part and water part', the essential identity of a hydrogel — its specific molecular architecture and the quantitative thresholds (>10% water, three-dimensional crosslinked polymer network) — cannot be expressed in everyday primes or grounded in the available catalog molecules (water, substance mixtures). It belongs to the chemistry/materials-engineering sector."}
<<<END:hydrogel>>>>

<<<KOT:idolatrously>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"Idolatrously is an adverbial form of idolatrous, which itself denotes a specific religious/cultural attitude (worship of idols, or metaphorically, excessive devotion to a person or idea). The adverb form requires the underlying concept of idolatry, which is culture-bound and theologically grounded. 'Worshipping an idol' involves a particular kind of religious practice and belief that is not decomposable into the prime lexicon (FEEL, THINK, DO, SEE, etc.) without losing its semantic core. The concept belongs to the religious/cultural sector, not the everyday semantic tier."}
<<<END:idolatrously>>>>
